tasks.instruction: Read the text below. Decide if each sentence (1.1-1.4) is True (T), False (F), or if the information is Not In the text (NI).
tasks.text:
A DAY AT THE BEACH

Last Saturday, my family went to the beach. We left home early in the morning because it takes about two hours to get there by car. My dad drove and my mum sat next to him. My brother and I sat in the back and played games on our tablets.

When we arrived, the weather was sunny and warm. We found a good spot near the water and set up our umbrella. My brother and I went swimming straight away. The water was a bit cold at first, but we got used to it quickly.

After swimming, we built a huge sandcastle together. It took us almost an hour! Then we had a picnic lunch — sandwiches, fruit, and lemonade. In the afternoon, my dad taught us how to play beach volleyball. It was really fun, even though I kept missing the ball. We left the beach at about five o'clock because my mum said we needed to get home before dark.

items.statement: The family arrived at the beach before lunchtime.
items.explanation: TRUE — They left early in the morning and the drive takes about two hours, so they arrived in the morning. They went swimming and built a sandcastle before having a picnic lunch.

items.statement: The children thought the sea water was too cold for swimming.
items.explanation: FALSE — The text says the water was 'a bit cold at first, but we got used to it quickly' — they went swimming.

items.statement: The family goes to this beach every weekend.
items.explanation: NO INFORMATION — The text only describes one trip ('Last Saturday'). It does not say how often they visit this beach.

items.statement: The narrator was good at playing beach volleyball.
items.explanation: FALSE — The text says 'I kept missing the ball', which shows the narrator was not good at it.
